title: Merch Eva DVD Question
wikitext:
[]: [Merch] Eva DVD Question             Quote Post by Quiddity » Sat Jan 23, 2010 5:57 pm Should hopefully be a nice, easy question! I'm looking to upgrade my Eva DVD collection to the directors cut episodes of 21 - 24 and was wondering if someone could confirm for me if the Platinum volume 6 disc (located here: http://www.amazon.com/Neon-Genesis-Evangelion-Platinum-Collection/dp/B0007D4ME0/ref=sr_1_23?ie=UTF8&s=dvd&qid=1264286972&sr=1-23) contains the directors cut versions of episodes 21 - 23. You'd think it'd be nice and easy, but nope, Amazon lists episodes 18 - 20 in the description and the reviews claim other episodes are there. @_@ Any help would be greatly appreciated!

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sat Jan 23, 2010 6:27 pm Both the initial director's cut DVDs (Resurrection and Genesis Reborn) and the standalone platinum volumes split 21'-23' and 24' from each other. I would recommend picking up the platinum thinpack. I realize that it may be a bit more than what you're intending, but if your intention is to "upgrade", then you will get the best quality out of the Thinpack, because it has 21'-24' on one disc and nothing else, and the bitrates for the episodes are better overall for the whole series. If you look around, chances are you can find a new copy for $50, even though it retails for $80. I assume you have the Perfect Collection? If you can find the Thinpack on sale, you really can't go wrong, it's a pretty good deal.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Jan 23, 2010 6:28 pm 21 through 23 On-air and DC are on Platinum Volume 6, Volume 7 has 24 (on air and DC) as well as episodes 25 and 26. Hope that helps (just checked my single volumes of Platinum so I know this for a fact). I guess what those are older volume reviews that people are referring to with episodes 18 through 20 or whatever it is.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Sat Jan 23, 2010 7:39 pm For some reason, the episode 23' on the original release (not part of platinum) has the image overlays in the Reiquarium, in platinum they were removed. Not that big of an issue.

[]:             Quote Post by GasmaskAvenger » Sat Jan 23, 2010 7:57 pm so the old non-platinum standalone release of episode 23' has the superimposed images? cool

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Jan 23, 2010 7:57 pm Oh, there's also the weirdness that the Renewal/Platinum On-air episodes aren't even On-Air proper. It's simply removing the DC footage from those episodes. Information on what I mean: http://wiki.evageeks.org/Guides:Episode_22_OA_vs._DC And this page has information of what Ornette's referring to: http://evacommentary.org/episode-23/23_reiquarium.html

[]:             Quote Post by GasmaskAvenger » Sat Jan 23, 2010 8:09 pm i've read that up a while ago, so I guess I need one more DVD to feel complete

[]:             Quote Post by Quiddity » Sun Jan 24, 2010 6:40 pm Thanks for the help. Right now I'm just interested in getting the DC episodes (which I've never had on DVD, believe it or not) and probably won't go through upgrading the entire series. I've always been happy with my Eva DVDs except for the fact that I only have the original broadcast versions of episodes 21-24.

[]:             Quote Post by tomrule123 » Sat Feb 27, 2010 3:18 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: Oh, there's also the weirdness that the Renewal/Platinum On-air episodes aren't even On-Air proper. It's simply removing the DC footage from those episodes. Information on what I mean: http://wiki.evageeks.org/Guides:Episode_22_OA_vs._DC And this page has information of what Ornette's referring to: http://evacommentary.org/episode-23/23_reiquarium.html

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sat Feb 27, 2010 3:51 pm No you don't. Just get the thin pack, and you're set. You get both the on air, and video version for 21-24.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Sat Feb 27, 2010 4:15 pm I don't have the thinpack, so I don't know. But the director's cuts in the thinpack has these overlays in the reiquarium scenes?

[]:             Quote Post by tomrule123 » Sat Feb 27, 2010 6:27 pm Ornette wrote: I don't have the thinpack, so I don't know. But the director's cuts in the thinpack has these overlays in the reiquarium scenes?

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Sat Feb 27, 2010 7:06 pm tomrule123 wrote: I have the thin pack and I don't recall seeing this image; and I saw that version a few times. I believe that it was removed during the restoration of Renewal of Evangelion.

[]:             Quote Post by tomrule123 » Sat Feb 27, 2010 11:15 pm Ornette wrote: tomrule123 wrote: I have the thin pack and I don't recall seeing this image; and I saw that version a few times. I believe that it was removed during the restoration of Renewal of Evangelion.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Sat Feb 27, 2010 11:28 pm Yeah. What I was originally asking was specific to the thinpack. I have the regular platinum discs and the 2 Director's Cut discs (got them before platinum was out). I know in the regular platinum collection the overlays are missing. Evamonkey mentioned that the thinpack was the only thing you need, so I asked if the thinpack platinum of that episode was somehow different than the regular platinum. I was lead to believe it was exactly the same, thus missing the overlays.

[]:             Quote Post by tomrule123 » Sat Feb 27, 2010 11:40 pm Ornette wrote: Yeah. What I was originally asking was specific to the thinpack. I have the regular platinum discs and the 2 Director's Cut discs (got them before platinum was out). I know in the regular platinum collection the overlays are missing. Evamonkey mentioned that the thinpack was the only thing you need, so I asked if the thinpack platinum of that episode was somehow different than the regular platinum. I was lead to believe it was exactly the same, thus missing the overlays.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Feb 27, 2010 11:48 pm The "Resurrection" DVD, includes #21-#23 OA/DC pre-remastering, does indeed have the overlay images.

[]:             Quote Post by tomrule123 » Sun Feb 28, 2010 11:21 am Reichu wrote: The "Resurrection" DVD, includes #21-#23 OA/DC pre-remastering, does indeed have the overlay images.

[]:             Quote Post by GentlemanX » Thu Jan 23, 2014 9:56 pm Since the rules said bumping threads was okay, I figured this was probably the best place to ask. I have the Platinum think pack but was curious what releases had the actual OA versions of 21-24 (the actual footage rather the reconstuction using Renewal footage in my set. For example, do the Platinum singles have the actual OA versions or are they just in the ordinal DVD and Resurrection releases) and what releases had 25 and 26's original dub. The dub of those episodes on the Platinum set I assume was made later since they have John Swasey voicing Gendo rather than Tristan Macavery. I'm also not sure if there are any other significant changes between the initial DVD release, the DC non-Platinum release, the Platinum singles, and the eventual Platinum set and would appreciate any more info about the differences for the actual episodes (I'm less concerned for example with ADVs commentaries and booklets, just differences to the episodes themselves or changes to the English dub). Thanks a lot!

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Jan 23, 2014 10:25 pm I don't fully understand your question, as all the Platinum releases have both versions of 21-24. Are you asking about the original broadcast versions?
